problem_statement: The GovernedPlanningProfile sequence has collected terminal closure certificate evidence and now needs a schema-backed runtime authorization request that submits the operator authorization question without granting runtime promotion, activation, execution, dispatch, replanning, success, or terminal closure authority.
success_criteria: a deterministic validator emits the runtime authorization request from the current terminal closure certificate
the checked-in local runtime authorization request matches the deterministic produced request
the schema records the authorization request as submitted while keeping operator response absent and runtime authorization gate unsatisfied
the request binds the terminal closure certificate id and hash and records all promotion evidence gates as satisfied
the request records that a separate signed runtime authorization response witness is required before activation

focused tests cover schema compatibility, fixture parity, source certificate binding, awaiting-response evidence, authority-drift rejection, collected-response drift rejection, unsatisfied source rejection, and boundary drift rejection
non_goals: do not record a signed operator response witness
do not execute, dispatch, replan, replay, or rollback
do not add a router, worker, scheduler, connector, or runtime registration path
do not claim live runtime activation
do not replace the terminal closure certificate
constraints: the runtime authorization request is local, read-only, deterministic, and reversible
operator response collection remains false until a separate signed response witness exists
runtime promotion activation remains denied until a separate authority-changing response and activation action are governed
Mfidel atomicity remains preserved because the validator does not normalize or decompose text
evidence_required: schema-backed local runtime authorization request fixture
deterministic validator and produced request parity
SDLC design and security review artifacts
